abstract: Pass/fail review gate for BEAM runtime architecture in Elixir systems: two blind reviewers judge a diff or file set against 23 decidable rules that catch wrong assumptions about the runtime's execution and delivery model — supervision and failure design, backpressure and overload, event delivery semantics, shared-state consistency, distribution reality, and runtime mechanics — each rule carrying the artifact evidence that decides PASS/FAIL/N/A with fail-closed carve-outs. Sibling of adversarial-elixir, which gates paradigm fit; judgment-call guidance lives in staff-level-elixir.
